checks.a_analysis: All text elements in the output (SSID descriptions and footer table content) are derived directly from the source document. The titles and metadata added for structure are within the scope of acceptable AI-generated enhancements.
The source document contains no contact information, and no fabricated phone numbers, emails, or URLs were introduced in the output.

The single page is represented in the output, including its main descriptive text and footer. Although significant text within the diagram is omitted (detailed in C5), the page as a whole is not missing.
The source footer table contains two rows of data. The output table contains two rows, preserving the information correctly despite minor cell alignment changes to normalize the grid.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The output fails to capture critical technical data blocks located within the central diagram. Specifically, the entire 'VLANs & Subnets' mapping (Employee – 210, agency; NCGovRoam – 410, guest; NCGov Guest – 410, guest) and the SSID visibility attributes (Employee – hidden; NCGovRoam – broadcast; NCGov Guest – broadcast) are omitted from both the text elements and the image alt text summary. This constitutes a loss of more than 25% of the page's unique technical specifications.

NCGovRoam – 410, guest
NCGov Guest – 410, guest

SSIDs
Employee – hidden
NCGovRoam – broadcast
NCGov Guest – broadcast
checks.b_evidence_output: (Text omitted from all paragraph and image alt text elements)
checks.b_evidence_location: Page 1, central diagram and middle-bottom text blocks

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: There are minor formatting differences, such as the omission of a space after the colon in 'Description:WLAN Service – Current', which are considered acceptable reformatting.

checks.a_analysis: The content from the source PDF appears once in the output JSON. There are no duplicated paragraphs, sections, or table data.

The source document contains a diagram and descriptive text without explicit formal headings. The output JSON correctly identifies zero headings, prioritizing the descriptive paragraphs and the footer table. No hierarchy is broken as no headings were defined.

The metadata table at the bottom of the source document has a 3-column structure (Date | Project | Created By in the top row; Department | Description | Seal space in the bottom row). The converted JSON table has 4 columns per row, adding unnecessary empty cells and misrepresenting the logical grid of the original document.
checks.b_evidence_source: The footer table visually contains three columns: column 1 for 'Date'/'Department', column 2 for 'Project'/'Description', and column 3 for 'Created By'/'Seal'.
checks.b_evidence_output: The output table rows contain 4 cells each: 'cells': [ { ... }, { ... }, { ... }, { ... } ]

checks.a_analysis: The output follows the logical top-to-bottom flow of the document: definitions of SSIDs first, followed by the network diagram (represented as an image), then the metadata table, and finally the state seal.

The SSID definitions at the top are formatted as a group of separate items in the source. The output renders these as separate paragraphs, which preserves the meaning and logical grouping correctly. No formal list structure (bullets/numbers) was present to be corrupted.
The source document contains no hyperlinks, and no links were fabricated or injected into the output.

checks.a_analysis: The source PDF is a single-page document dominated by a complex network diagram illustrating connections between users, access points, and various network resources. The HTML output completely fails to reproduce this layout. It strips away all the spatial relationships, arrows, and icons that define the document's structure, leaving only isolated text blocks and a table. The visual organization of information is fundamentally lost.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF page 1 features a network topology diagram with users on the left, an access point in the center, and networks/servers on the right, connected by labeled arrows.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML screenshot shows a linear list of text paragraphs followed by a table. The graphical layout of the network diagram is missing entirely, replaced by unrelated green placeholder blocks.

checks.a_analysis: The table at the bottom of the document containing metadata (Date, Project, etc.) is rendered correctly in the HTML. It maintains a logical row/column structure with visible borders and is easily readable.

The source document relies heavily on meaningful imagery, including icons for 'Guests' and 'State Employees', cloud graphics for 'Internet Access' and 'State Network', icons for servers and access points, and the official North Carolina state seal. All of these meaningful visual elements are missing from the HTML output. The output contains two green boxes that do not correspond to any meaningful content from the source.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 1 contains icons for users, network clouds, hardware icons for servers/APs, and the Great Seal of the State of North Carolina.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML screenshot lacks all icons and the state seal. The 'diagram' area contains abstract green shapes that convey no information.

checks.a_analysis: I have reviewed the HTML output and compared it to the source. No body content, paragraphs, or tables appear to be duplicated in the rendered view.
